mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 6 months signals tightening monetary policy that raises the discount rate on equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as valuations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS rose from near-zero in 2015 to 2.19% by Oct 2018 in a documented hiking cycle
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing 6m FEDFUNDS change and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive-return share in rising-rate periods exceeds the unconditional 65.5% base rate.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window after a period of historic lows, it signals a labor market inflection that precedes earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over trailing 3 months from a cycle low
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% Oct-2018, near multi-decade low, leaving limited room for further improvement
failure_condition: In triggered episodes, forward 20d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the effect is not statistically distinguishable from zero at p>0.10 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in HOUST and RSAFS growth over the trailing 6 months indicates simultaneous weakening in housing investment and consumer spending, two independent demand pillars, and this combined deceleration predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days more strongly than either series alone.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST fell from 1250(revised) toward 1201 vintage reading by Sep-2018 amid rising mortgage rates
failure_condition: The joint HOUST-RSAFS deceleration signal's Rank IC with 60d forward returns is not meaningfully higher (by >0.02) than the max of the two individual single-series Rank ICs across a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a deceleration in year-over-year INDPRO growth below its trailing 12-month average signals overextended late-cycle capacity utilization giving way to slower output, which precedes negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as forward earnings expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: YoY INDPRO growth falls below its trailing 12-month average while FEDFUNDS is rising
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage/revision gap (108.48 vs revised 104.10) shows measurement noise around late-cycle production peak in 2018
failure_condition: During documented tightening cycles with INDPRO deceleration, forward 20d SPX returns average non-negative, or hypothesis Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows restricted to tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 2.0% for the first time in a hiking cycle, it marks a psychological and mechanical threshold at which short-term borrowing costs begin materially compressing corporate margins, triggering a sharp negative S&P 500 reaction over the following 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 2.00% for first time in current hiking cycle
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS reached 2.19% by Oct-2018 vintage, having crossed 2.0% earlier in 2018
failure_condition: Mean 5d forward SPX return following the 2.0% crossing event is not significantly negative (p>0.10) across the sample of hiking cycles observed since 2005.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive month-over-month growth in PAYEMS over the trailing 12 months reflects durable labor income expansion that supports consumption and corporate revenue, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage 149,750k vs revised 149,527k reflects continued payroll expansion trend through Oct-2018
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing 12m PAYEMS growth and forward 60d SPX return is below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or effect reverses sign in more than one distinct historical subperiod.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL implies a shrinking or negative real policy accommodation gap, which jointly signals tightening real financial conditions beyond what either nominal rate or inflation level indicates alone, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.19% and CPIAUCSL at 252.827 (Oct-2018 vintage) imply modest positive but narrowing real rate gap as hikes continue
failure_condition: The interaction term (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL YoY inflation) shows no incremental Rank IC (<0.02 improvement) over the single-series FEDFUNDS model across a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a decline in HOUST of more than 10% from its trailing 12-month peak signals rate-sensitive demand destruction in housing that leads broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% from trailing 12-month peak while FEDFUNDS is above 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage 1201k, revised 1250k, both below early-2018 highs amid FEDFUNDS at 2.19%
failure_condition: In episodes meeting the HOUST decline trigger under tightening conditions, forward 60d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 3-year evaluation windows restricted to tightening regimes.
